# Chapter 5: Sensors and instruments  (~90 pp)

- 5.1 What a sensor is (8)
- 5.2 Transducer principles: thermal, electrical, optical, mechanical, chemical, magnetic (20)
- 5.3 Sensor specifications: accuracy, precision, resolution, range, drift, hysteresis (18)
- 5.4 Choosing the right sensor (12)
- 5.5 Common instruments: multimeter, oscilloscope, micrometer, scale, microscope, sensor arrays (20)
- 5.6 Failure section: when the sensor lies, and why it usually lies in the same direction (12)

**Archetype**: interface, transport (signal from physical phenomenon to data).
**Project**: build a simple sensor (thermistor + voltage divider + ADC) and characterise it.
**Exercises**: 30.

---

Volume 1 dossier: [`_volume.md`](_volume.md). Chapter shell: `volumes/01-quantity/ch05-sensors-and-instruments/chapter.tex`.
